whole_transcript:
Therapist: Good morning! How are you feeling today?
Patient: My neck is still pretty stiff, honestly.
Therapist: Okay, well, we have a long session planned today. First, I want to administer a physical performance test to see where your baseline is at right now. We'll take our time with it.
Patient: Sounds good.
[45 minutes of physical performance testing - 97750]
Therapist: Alright, let's loosen up that neck. We're going to do some gentle therapeutic exercises. Let's start by doing some basic range of motion exercises.
[7 minutes of therapeutic exercises - 97110]
Therapist: Good. Now I'm going to do some manual therapy on you. Just a little cervical joint mobilization to help with that stiffness.
[7 minutes of manual therapy - 97140]
Therapist: Okay, I want you to head over to the main gym area. You'll join the others for some group therapy. They're doing some group exercises right now.
[30 minutes of group therapy - 97150]
Therapist: Step out of the group for a second, let's do some one-on-one therapeutic activities. We really need to work on your transfer training from the mat.
[30 minutes of therapeutic activities - 97530]
Therapist: You're doing great. Now we're moving into the advanced stuff. We're going to spend an hour working on work conditioning and work hardening activities simulating your desk setup.
[60 minutes of work hardening add-on - 97546]
Therapist: Let's give you a break. I'm going to apply mechanical traction to decompress your spine.
[30 minutes of mechanical traction - 97012]
Therapist: And while you're on the traction table, I'm also going to set up unattended electrical stimulation on your lower back.
[30 minutes of unattended e-stim - 97014]
Therapist: Actually, I also want you doing some progressive resistance exercises with your arms while you're laying there. We're going to push hard for the last hour to get these therapeutic exercises in.
[60 minutes of therapeutic exercises - 97110]
